mnemonics.title: Alphabet Definition
mnemonics.content: A - Alphabet: Always finite, non-empty set of symbols. Remember: 'A' stands for 'Atomic' - cannot be broken down further.

mnemonics.title: DFA vs NFA
mnemonics.content: D-Deterministic has '1 way' (Exactly one transition). N-Non-deterministic has 'N-ways' (Multiple or zero transitions).

mnemonics.title: Kleene Star vs Plus
mnemonics.content: Star (*) means 'Zero or More' (like '*' key on phone - dial any number). Plus (+) means 'One or More' (like '+' on medal - at least one).

mnemonics.title: Regular Expression Operators
mnemonics.content: Priority: Parentheses > Star/Plus > Concatenation > Union (|). Remember: Please Excuse My Dear Aunt Sally but replace with |.

acronyms.usage: Abstract machine with finite states that processes input strings
FA where each state has exactly one transition per input symbol
FA where transitions can be to multiple states or use ε-moves
Pattern notation to describe regular languages
Finite set of input symbols in automata theory
Function defining state transitions in an automaton
Finite non-empty set of states in an automaton
Subset of Q containing accepting states

visualTips.visualization: Draw circles for states, arrows for transitions labeled with input symbols. Start state has incoming arrow with 'Start' label. Final states have double circles.
Trace a path from start state following input symbols. If you end in a final state, string is accepted. Use finger to trace the path.
Think of star as 'repeating' - a* means you can repeat 'a' any number of times including zero (ε). Draw as: ε → a → aa → aaa → ...
Alphabet (Σ) → Strings (Σ*) → Language (subset of Σ*). Like: Letters → Words → Valid Sentences.

analogies.analogy: Think of a turnstile at a metro station: locked (one state), unlocked (another state). It transitions based on coin insertion (input).
Alphabet symbols are like LEGO bricks. Strings are LEGO structures. Language is a collection of valid structures you can build.
From each location (state), GPS gives exactly one next direction for each choice - deterministic, always knows the next step.
At each step, NFA can 'guess' the right path - like having multiple friends try different routes to find a working one.
RE is like a wildcard pattern: a*b matches 'ab', 'aab', 'aaab', etc., similar to file search patterns like '*.txt'.
